description_long: This is a roughly hewn stone slab featuring a prominent relief carving of a draped hand holding a sphere topped with a cross, suggestive of a symbolic or religious motif. The stone is irregular in shape, with a textured, weathered surface and visible chisel marks, indicating it may have been hand-carved. The front face is the most detailed, while the sides and back are rough and unfinished, with the rear mounted against a dark wooden or metal backing plate. The coloration is primarily a pale, off-white with subtle brownish and grayish streaks, likely from natural mineral deposits or aging. The object appears solid and heavy, with no visible cavities or openings. Its form and mounting suggest it could serve as a decorative or commemorative barrier in an urban park, cemetery, or historical site.
description: A carved stone slab with a relief of a draped hand holding a cross-topped sphere, mounted on a dark backing. The surface is rough and weathered, with pale and earthy tones.
description_view_0: The front view shows the detailed relief carving of a hand holding a sphere with a cross, with the draped fabric and textured stone surface clearly visible.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the stone's irregular thickness and rough, unfinished surface, as well as the depth of the relief carving.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the dark, flat backing plate that supports the stone, with minimal detail on the stone itself.
description_view_3: The right side view highlights the protrusion of the carved elements and the stone's uneven, natural edge.
attribute_placement: mounted upright on a backing plate, set as a standalone feature
attribute_pattern: Features a carved relief pattern of a draped hand and sphere with a cross; surface shows irregular chisel marks and natural stone veining.
attribute_weathering: Surface is rough and weathered, with visible discoloration and minor erosion, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with man-made structures due to its organic shape and weathered appearance; mounting plate allows for secure placement against walls or as a freestanding monument.
